scope.summary: Extend the Glue/Tack placement handler to validate and store multi-point tack arrays, enforce per-tack r_local derivation from tack_anchor_json defaults when tack positions are unspecified, and expose the multi-anchor authoring path in the operator UI and MCP tool input schema
acceptance_criteria: When tack anchor positions are omitted, the handler reads tack_anchor_json from tailor_trims and uses those body-local offsets as the default r_local for each GpuTackConstraint per TR-6.1.5
Multi-point tack placements (e.g., 4-hole button with 4 tacks) generate exactly N GpuTackConstraint entries where N = length of tacks_json array
TACK_SEAM_CLOSURE validation check (TR-11.1) correctly evaluates all N tack distances at end of draping
TailorTrimTackUpdated event is emitted when an existing placement's tacks_json is modified
The place_trim MCP tool (MT-263) input schema accepts an optional tack_anchors field as a list of panel-local UV positions
scope.risk_if_missed: Four-hole buttons, rivets, and any trim with multiple attachment points will silently use a single default tack, producing physically incorrect simulation (trim rotates freely instead of being pinned at all holes); TACK_SEAM_CLOSURE check cannot be meaningful with only one constraint per multi-anchor trim

risks: Broad MT scope can hide multiple review targets.
Markdown MT lists may disagree with packet-declared active/next MT truth.
Receipt routing may target an old MT if contract identity is omitted.
minimum_controls: Exactly one primary proof target per MT unless the packet records why not.
MT identity must be stable and referenced by receipt correlation.
Generated projection drift must not wake Coder or Validator by itself.
